patches.find: Verdict boundary note: WIN-006 uses “Std Model Explains” rather than “Misleading” (WIN-007, WIN-022) because the pre-1990 linear drift is accurately described at face value — both models fit the same linear trend, so there is no false causal claim, only an absence of discriminating power. WIN-007 and WIN-022 are “Misleading” because they introduce specific causal framings (“aetheric threshold crossing,” “phase transition”) for the post-1990 acceleration that are category errors: the dome asserts a mechanistic explanation it cannot derive, not merely a shared observation.
patches.replace: Verdict boundary note: WIN-006 uses “Std Model Explains” rather than “Misleading” (WIN-007, WIN-022) despite all three sharing the same parent prediction (PROS-002: “aetheric vortex precession below threshold”). The distinction is not that WIN-006 lacks a mechanism claim in the parent — it does not; the dome names aetheric vortex for both the pre-1990 linear phase and the post-1990 acceleration. The distinction is in the type of language inside each WIN’s own row text: WIN-006 is observation-focused (“both models fit the same linear trend”), while WIN-007 and WIN-022 foreground borrowed thermodynamic labels (“aetheric threshold crossing,” “phase transition”) that import false specificity the dome cannot back up — no order parameter, no critical exponent, no symmetry-breaking mechanism is specified. A claim that “both models fit the slope” is non-discriminating and not misleading; a claim that the post-1990 acceleration is a “phase transition” asserts a testable mechanistic type the dome has not derived from its own geometry.
patches.rationale: WIN-006.c5 curmudgeon review: boundary note is fragile because it says WIN-006 has 'no false causal claim' while PROS-002 parent explicitly invokes 'aetheric vortex' for the pre-1990 drift. Rewritten to locate the distinction in the WIN-row language (observation-focused vs. thermodynamic-label-heavy) rather than absence-of-mechanism-in-parent, per curmudgeon option (a).
